# Blueprint v0.3.1 — UX fixes from first end-to-end testing

Four fixes for issues reported during the first real user test of
v0.3.0.

## Stop button in the Verify chat

The Deploy → Verify chat had no way to interrupt a streaming
response. Once llama-server started generating, you watched until it
finished or the max-tokens cap kicked in. Now the Send button swaps
to a square Stop button while a response is streaming; clicking it
aborts the fetch (via AbortController) and keeps whatever streamed
so far.

DashboardChat already had this; the bug was that the
just-finished-deploy chat surface — the one a brand-new user hits
first — didn't.

## Fit-vs-your-GPU badge on every model card in the Plan tab

Walked the planner with a brand-new user — they had no signal for
"which of these can I actually run on my laptop?" The cards now
fetch the live system snapshot and display a colored pill per model:

  ● **runs on this machine**  (totalGB ≤ 70% of detected VRAM)
  ● **tight fit**             (70–100% — works but no headroom)
  ● **needs more VRAM**       (exceeds detected VRAM at this quant)

A header line at the top of the model list confirms the detected
VRAM number so the badges are interpretable. Hidden entirely on
hosts with no GPU detected.

## Prominent "Back to Dashboard" button in the wizard

The TitleBar's "← Back to Dashboard" button (visible whenever the
wizard is open) was a subtle outline button that blended into the
chrome. After clicking through Plan → Hardware → Deploy and
launching a model, the natural next move is to return to the
Dashboard to interact with it — but testers couldn't find their way
back. Promoted to primary-colored, semibold, with shadow.

## Graceful crash-state recovery, with specific guidance for
"llama-server not found"

When the supervisor entered crash-loop state, the dashboard rendered
a destructive-red wall titled **"Child keeps crashing"** with only a
"Restart" button as recourse. Brand-corrosive on first launch.

Two changes:

1. **Calmer visual tone.** Crash state now uses amber-warning
   styling instead of destructive red. Headline reads "Supervisor
   stopped — Model server keeps exiting" — same information,
   different emotional register.
2. **Specific guidance for the most common cause.** When `lastError`
   matches patterns like "llama-server not found", "file not found",
   or "runtime not installed", the headline becomes **"Runtime not
   installed — llama-server isn't on disk yet"** and the primary
   action becomes **"Install runtime →"** (calls the existing
   `InstallRuntime` IPC directly). Once installed, the supervisor's
   next 5-second cycle picks up the binary and starts serving.
3. **"Stop trying" escape hatch.** Added a secondary button that
   calls `StopManagedServer` so the user can break the crash loop
   without uninstalling anything — useful when they want to apply a
   different model config instead of retrying the broken one.

## Upgrade path

Same procedure as any update — download the v0.3.1 installer from
[llmblueprint.ai/download](https://llmblueprint.ai/download). Models
and configs in `~/.blueprint/` are preserved.
